entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop whose germplasm seed viability was studied after storage.

entities.name: SEED VIABILITY
entities.description: The capability of seeds to germinate after a period of storage.

entities.name: STORAGE
entities.description: The act of keeping seeds for ten years as a base collection.

entities.name: BASE COLLECTION
entities.description: The purpose of the seed storage for long-term preservation.

entities.name: BOTANICAL VARIETIES
entities.description: The different botanical classifications of the groundnut accessions.

entities.name: PATANCHERU, ANDHRA PRADESH, INDIA
entities.description: The location where the groundnut seeds were stored.

entities.name: REGENERATION REQUIREMENTS
entities.description: The requirements needed to regenerate the stored seeds.

relationships.description: The study assesses the seed viability of groundnut accessions.
Groundnut seeds were subjected to ten years of storage.
Ten years of storage impacted the seed germination capability.
The storage of seeds occurred in Patancheru, Andhra Pradesh, India.
Groundnut accessions were categorized by different botanical varieties.
The paper provides notes on the regeneration requirements for groundnut seeds.
The storage was conducted as part of a base collection.
